Dashboard.Interactive:
System design notes
- Flagships set the point of view and topical authority.
- Clusters turn that authority into practical workflows and proof-backed guidance.
- Interactive assets create memorable, hard-to-copy entry points.
- Selective scalable templates expand coverage only where page-level user value is clear.
- Existing rajeevg.com proof pages should be used as trust-transfer assets across the whole system.

Master_Matrix.Working_Title: The AI-native delivery stack for data, media, and analytics teams

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: Why to use AI + web search (due to training data limitations content coverage/cut-off, hallucinations and for specific data access e.g. API docs)
Title_Decisions.Issue: Strong insight but framed as a generic explainer; needs a sharper operator-focused promise.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: Why AI + web search beats closed-model prompting for real work
Title_Decisions.Notes: Use concrete examples: API docs, live product data, fast-moving policy changes, analytics/debugging workflows.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: Simulating APIs with CSVs etc (helpful for people working with sensitive data, who need dummy data for testing, synthetic data, etc)
Title_Decisions.Issue: Good practical hook; broaden from CSVs to safe interface simulation and synthetic data workflows.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: Simulating APIs with CSVs, fixtures, and synthetic data when production access is risky
Title_Decisions.Notes: Include patterns for QA, demos, stakeholder reviews, and agent-safe development.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: Agents.md! And how utterly powerful it is if you give it the paths to files that contain: tool maps and doc on how to use them. The agents file simply tells the agent when it should use each tool an where it can find the tools capabilities, instructions, readiness status, etc
Title_Decisions.Issue: Powerful idea but too informal and too file-name specific for a top-level title.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: Agents.md, tool maps, and workflow docs: how to make coding agents reliably useful
Title_Decisions.Notes: Explain the design pattern, not just the file. Show readiness states, routing rules, and failure modes.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: How the process of Feasibility and documentation research + optimised prompt + build reduces risk of undesirable LLM results
Title_Decisions.Issue: Concept is strong but title is long and awkward.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: The feasibility -> documentation -> prompt -> build loop that reduces LLM failure
Title_Decisions.Notes: Frame as risk reduction and prototype quality control.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: What are tokens, what is context: Context window management and why! Optimal tasks sizing for llms, when to use large vs small prompts and why
Title_Decisions.Issue: Too broad for one post; combine theory, heuristics, and tooling.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: Tokens, context windows, and task sizing: how to stop wasting model context
Title_Decisions.Notes: Pair editorial explanation with a context budget calculator and chunking examples.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: From vibe coding to agentic engineering, platforms techniques, suitability (from bolt and replit to coding agents with tool use)
Title_Decisions.Issue: Very strong strategic topic; needs a cleaner title and clearer comparison framework.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: From vibe coding to agentic engineering
Title_Decisions.Notes: Should become a signature point-of-view page with frameworks, not just trend commentary.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: How chatgpt uses tools and code execution to do clever stuff and get deterministic results (code interpreter, agent skills)
Title_Decisions.Issue: Potentially too product-specific unless framed around tool-using assistants more broadly.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: How tool-using AI assistants get more deterministic results
Title_Decisions.Notes: Use ChatGPT as a concrete example, but teach the more general model/tool/code-execution pattern.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: The limits and risks of vibe coding, tool use (rce, prompt injection)
Title_Decisions.Issue: Important but should not read as fear content.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: The real risks of vibe coding: prompt injection, unsafe tool use, and false confidence
Title_Decisions.Notes: Anchor in practical mitigation patterns and review checklists.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: Progression of LLMs from basic text generation to sophisticated software automation (larger context windows, reasoning, tool use) Evolution of LLM usefulness over time (from text generation, to multimodal, to tool-use)
Title_Decisions.Issue: Useful background, but too history-heavy as a standalone page.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: Merge into: From vibe coding to agentic engineering
Title_Decisions.Notes: Best used as a short visual timeline, not a separate SEO target.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: How to leverage how LLMs generate text- (literally just ask them to categorise their claims by: proven fact- strong inference- weak inference / unresolved ambiguity)
Title_Decisions.Issue: Excellent original angle; needs clearer framing.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: A simple framework for making LLM outputs more trustworthy: fact, inference, or ambiguity?
Title_Decisions.Notes: This is differentiating because it converts prompting into epistemic hygiene.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: Levels of AI adoption/maturity (from using a chatbot, to orchestrating a wide array of tools, software, workflows MCP, CLI, CPI, agent skill, agents md, tool maps, workflow docs, to a fully automated setup - symphony/ralph loops)
Title_Decisions.Issue: Strong strategic topic; should become a visual framework and diagnostic.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: The AI adoption maturity model for operator teams
Title_Decisions.Notes: A strong candidate for a signature visual asset and lead magnet later.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: Levels of automation adoptions/maturity (from in-platform automations codex automations etc > Zapier/N8N > launchd / CRON / Windows Task Scheduler+Windows services)
Title_Decisions.Issue: Good companion concept; should be narrower than the AI maturity model.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: The automation maturity ladder: from no-code triggers to durable background jobs
Title_Decisions.Notes: Useful because it ties tooling choice to operational reliability.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: When is AI generated content okay? When is it not? (Generating code verifiable code with guardrails - ok, generating social media content - not ok)
Title_Decisions.Issue: Topic is crowded; the value must come from a sharp operator lens.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: When AI-generated output is acceptable - and when it quietly destroys trust
Title_Decisions.Notes: Differentiate by focusing on verifiability, reviewability, and consequence of error.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: Anatomy of a good prompt, this is an example of a good prompt that produced the desired outcome first time...
Title_Decisions.Issue: Too long and example-bound in its current form.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: How to write build prompts that produce working software, not impressive-looking code
Title_Decisions.Notes: Use the Apps Script + CM360 example as a teardown case, not as the title.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: Be an orchestrator, not a coder (match up existing technologies (GitHub repos) with business use cases/pain points - instead of building tech from scratch build solutions) dont reinvent the wheel, etc
Title_Decisions.Issue: Very strong commercial point of view; should be elevated.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: Be an orchestrator, not a coder
Title_Decisions.Notes: Important for attracting business-minded operators, not just hobbyist builders.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: Token efficiency, how to offload context intensive tasks to cheaper agents (context brokers)
Title_Decisions.Issue: Great advanced topic; better as a deeper supporting page than a top-level pillar.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: Context brokers and token efficiency: how to offload context-heavy work to cheaper agents
Title_Decisions.Notes: This is distinctive because it is operational, economic, and technical at once.

Title_Decisions.Raw_title: When to use an automation/tool vs. When to use an agent (with tools/automations)
Title_Decisions.Issue: Strong decision-framework topic; ideal for interactive treatment.
Title_Decisions.Recommended_title: When to use an automation, a tool, or an agent
Title_Decisions.Notes: Likely to perform well for latent need states, not just exact-match search.

Topic_Graph.Description: Umbrella operating-model topic for how serious teams use AI to deliver work.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: The delivery-stack hub should point users into the build-mode and agent-system detail pages.

Topic_Graph.Description: Build-system topic covering tool-using agents, build modes, and delivery workflows.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Business value and orchestration provide the commercial rationale for the stack.

Topic_Graph.Description: Trust, research, verification, and truthfulness patterns.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Maturity framing helps visitors self-locate before diving into tactics.

Topic_Graph.Description: How to choose and scale automation mechanisms.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Agentic engineering pages should repeatedly reference agents.md, tool maps, and workflow docs.

Topic_Graph.Description: Measurement-system design, contracts, consent, first-party delivery, warehousing.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: The feasibility/documentation loop is a risk-control mechanism for agentic delivery.

Topic_Graph.Description: Application layer that ties AI and automation into paid media operations.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Risk/gov content provides counterweight and trust.

Topic_Graph.Description: Commercial and operational rationale for solution stitching over greenfield building.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Research and verification practices become inputs to the build loop.

Topic_Graph.Description: Scope sizing, context budgets, and task decomposition.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Truthfulness labeling is a concrete sub-framework inside reliable AI usage.

Topic_Graph.Description: Reusable routing and capability-disclosure layer for agents.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Reliable AI usage principles make agentic pages more credible and reusable.

Topic_Graph.Description: Risk-reducing delivery loop before generation.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Mechanism selection is the center of the automation cluster.

Topic_Graph.Description: Mechanism-selection logic for workflows.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: The maturity ladder gives structure to automation architecture.

Topic_Graph.Description: Signature maturity model for teams.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Many visitors will move from automation questions into agent questions.

Topic_Graph.Description: Operational reliability ladder for automation tooling.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: The analytics implementation page validates the measurement-system pillar.

Topic_Graph.Description: Prompt injection, unsafe tool use, false confidence, policy boundaries.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Contracts and schemas are the durable center of measurement architecture.

Topic_Graph.Description: Output truthfulness labeling framework.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Paid media teams are a concrete high-value audience for analytics + AI systems.

Topic_Graph.Description: Annotated build prompts and outcomes.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: The CM360/App Script example is a specific proof pattern for the paid media audience.

Topic_Graph.Description: Niche workflow bridge into ad tech and spreadsheet operations.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Selective scalable templates turn the orchestrator view into discoverable assets.

Topic_Graph.Description: Measurement schema design pattern.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Good prompt/build loops depend on task sizing and context management.

Topic_Graph.Description: Existing implementation proof that transfers trust to analytics pages.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: The calculator is a practical interactive manifestation of the context topic.

Topic_Graph.Description: Diagrams, decision tools, calculators, diagnostics, benchmark explorers.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: The architecture explorer should visualize the routing/configuration layer.

Topic_Graph.Description: Curated comparison, playbook, pattern, and glossary templates.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: Prompt teardowns show how the loop performs in practice.

Topic_Graph.Description: Structured resource pages that add machine-readable authority.
Topic_Graph.Why_it_matters: The automation-vs-agent decision tree operationalizes the framework.

Topic_Graph.Description: The maturity model becomes more memorable and useful as a diagnostic.
Programmatic expansion must stay within people-first and anti-spam rules.
Annotated prompt examples can evolve into downloadable datasets and benchmark inputs.
Interactive tools can hand users into curated comparison/playbook/pattern pages.
Scalable pages should always ladder into flagship authority pages.
Datasets and benchmarks raise trust and machine-readable authority for AI-related claims.

Programmatic.Why_valid: Only when workflow materially changes the recommendation. 'for spreadsheet automation' should differ from 'for prototype UI generation'.
Programmatic.Required_data_model: tool_a, tool_b, workflow, team_type, constraints, review burden, speed_to_first_output, control_level, risk_profile, proof_links, best_for, not_for
Programmatic.Unique_value_required: Workflow fit narrative; evidence-backed tradeoffs; recommendation summary; links to proof and alternatives
Programmatic.Canonical_and_indexing: Canonical to the primary comparison URL; noindex faceted filters and low-delta combinations; index only hand-reviewed pages
Programmatic.Structured_data: Article + Breadcrumb; optionally SoftwareApplication on comparison explorer, not on every page
Programmatic.Primary_risk: Thin 'X vs Y' pages, stale vendor data, feature-parity spam
Programmatic.Operational_note: Treat these as editorially-curated templates, not full combinatorial scale.

Programmatic.Why_valid: Valid because paid media managers, analysts, and measurement engineers have different constraints, approval paths, and KPIs for the same workflow.
Programmatic.Required_data_model: role, workflow, inputs, tools, approval_steps, data_sensitivity, outputs, QA_checks, KPI_targets, linked_templates
Programmatic.Unique_value_required: Concrete SOP-like steps, example prompts, tool stack suggestions, review checklist, expected outputs
Programmatic.Canonical_and_indexing: Canonical self-reference; noindex thin tails; index only combinations with original examples and screenshots
Programmatic.Primary_risk: Templated fluff and repetitive role pages with minimal differentiation

Programmatic.Why_valid: Useful because users often know the pain point but not the implementation category.
Programmatic.Required_data_model: problem, stack, prerequisites, data_inputs, outputs, failure_modes, proof_assets, related_workflows, effort, maintenance_cost
Programmatic.Unique_value_required: Named pattern, best-use conditions, caveats, linked proof pages, implementation notes
Programmatic.Canonical_and_indexing: Canonical self-reference; index only patterns backed by proof; noindex speculative or placeholder patterns
Programmatic.Primary_risk: Keyword-driven pattern inflation without real evidence
Programmatic.Operational_note: Excellent promptless SEO fit when grounded in real recurring problems.

Programmatic.Why_valid: Valid for entities that recur across multiple pages and genuinely confuse readers or AI systems.
Programmatic.Required_data_model: entity_name, entity_type, plain_definition, why_it_matters, adjacent_entities, examples, related_pages
Programmatic.Unique_value_required: Short definition plus business relevance, comparisons, examples, and linked next steps
Programmatic.Canonical_and_indexing: Canonical self-reference; index only top entities; noindex stubs or duplicate definitions
Programmatic.Primary_risk: Mass-generated definitions with no workflow context
Programmatic.Operational_note: Good support layer for AI-search entity grounding, not a volume play.

Programmatic.Why_valid: Valid where the site is publishing original benchmark slices or downloadable structured resources.
Programmatic.Required_data_model: benchmark_name, methodology, update_date, variables, downloadable_files, caveats, related_pages
Programmatic.Unique_value_required: Methodology transparency, downloadable data, interpretation notes, visuals, update log
Programmatic.Canonical_and_indexing: Canonical to dataset landing page; list pages can be noindex if purely navigational
Programmatic.Primary_risk: Publishing shallow tables without interpretation or provenance
Programmatic.Operational_note: Best used sparingly for recurring original evidence.

Programmatic.Why_valid: Valid because calculators, diagnostics, and decision tools can solve a problem better than prose alone.
Programmatic.Required_data_model: tool_name, inputs, outputs, formulas_or_rules, examples, related_guides, static_copy
Programmatic.Unique_value_required: Crawlable explanatory copy, worked examples, methodology, downloadable worksheet/static fallback
Programmatic.Canonical_and_indexing: Canonical self-reference; index if useful as a standalone destination; noindex experiments or thin MVPs
Programmatic.Structured_data: SoftwareApplication + Breadcrumb; optionally VideoObject on walkthrough pages
Programmatic.Primary_risk: JS-heavy pages with no crawlable explanatory content
Programmatic.Operational_note: Each tool needs a substantial non-JS fallback and clear educational value.

Interactive_Assets.User_problem_solved: Clarifies how prompts, docs, tools, memory, validation, and human checkpoints fit together.
Interactive_Assets.SEO_or_discovery_benefit: Can earn links, citations, and keep advanced readers on-site longer.
Interactive_Assets.Conversion_or_retention_benefit: High retention; supports multiple flagship essays; memorable brand asset.
Interactive_Assets.Underlying_data_or_content_model: Nodes for agent, tool map, workflow doc, readiness state, verifier, human review, output artifact; edge metadata for data/control flow.
Interactive_Assets.Crawlable_fallback_requirement: Include a static annotated diagram image, full text explanation, and node-by-node HTML copy.

Interactive_Assets.User_problem_solved: Answers the practical 'what should I use here?' question faster than prose.
Interactive_Assets.SEO_or_discovery_benefit: Captures latent workflow intent and can surface for comparison-style queries.
Interactive_Assets.Conversion_or_retention_benefit: High return use and strong handoff into playbooks and tools.
Interactive_Assets.Underlying_data_or_content_model: Decision criteria such as ambiguity, repetition, approval need, tolerance for error, latency sensitivity, and maintenance burden.
Interactive_Assets.Crawlable_fallback_requirement: Provide the full decision logic in crawlable HTML beneath the tree.

Interactive_Assets.User_problem_solved: Helps users estimate whether a task fits a prompt/model/routing strategy before wasting cycles.
Interactive_Assets.SEO_or_discovery_benefit: Strong utility-led discovery and repeat visits.
Interactive_Assets.Conversion_or_retention_benefit: Practical daily-use asset for advanced users.
Interactive_Assets.Underlying_data_or_content_model: Inputs for prompt length, docs/files, overhead, compression ratio, safety margin, desired output size.
Interactive_Assets.Crawlable_fallback_requirement: Render a worked-example table and formulas in HTML so the page is useful without the calculator UI.

Interactive_Assets.User_problem_solved: Turns an abstract framework into a reusable assessment for teams.
Interactive_Assets.SEO_or_discovery_benefit: Shareable framework asset that can attract strategic audiences.
Interactive_Assets.Conversion_or_retention_benefit: High retention and future lead value if paired with downloadable scorecard.
Interactive_Assets.Underlying_data_or_content_model: Questions mapped to maturity stages, weighted scoring, recommendation modules, next-step content links.
Interactive_Assets.Crawlable_fallback_requirement: Provide a static worksheet version and a written explanation of each maturity stage.

Interactive_Assets.User_problem_solved: Lets users compare build approaches by workflow, not just vendor marketing categories.
Interactive_Assets.SEO_or_discovery_benefit: Captures valuable mid-funnel discovery and supports selective comparison pages.
Interactive_Assets.Conversion_or_retention_benefit: Useful recurring asset that deepens the site's distinctiveness.
Interactive_Assets.Underlying_data_or_content_model: Tool entities, criteria matrix, workflow tags, evidence notes, recommendation weights, linked proof pages.
Interactive_Assets.Crawlable_fallback_requirement: Expose the comparison summaries, criteria explanations, and key tables in crawlable HTML.

Interactive_Assets.User_problem_solved: Shows how consent, tagging, first-party transport, sGTM, GA4, warehousing, and reporting connect.
Interactive_Assets.SEO_or_discovery_benefit: Useful for analytics queries where diagrams outperform prose.
Interactive_Assets.Conversion_or_retention_benefit: Raises trust for the analytics cluster and encourages multi-page sessions.
Interactive_Assets.Underlying_data_or_content_model: System nodes, event flows, implementation notes, screenshots, glossary hooks.
Interactive_Assets.Crawlable_fallback_requirement: Pair with a static architecture image, textual sequence description, and linked sub-sections.

Interactive_Assets.User_problem_solved: Shows which parts of a build prompt drive outcome quality: objective, context, constraints, files, outputs, setup, acceptance criteria.
Interactive_Assets.SEO_or_discovery_benefit: Can attract top-of-funnel prompt curiosity without becoming generic advice.
Interactive_Assets.Conversion_or_retention_benefit: Strong teaching asset and shareable reference.
Interactive_Assets.Underlying_data_or_content_model: Prompt sections, annotations, examples, success/failure commentary.
Interactive_Assets.Crawlable_fallback_requirement: Ensure the full annotated prompt is visible in HTML and downloadable as text.

Interactive_Assets.User_problem_solved: Makes benchmark data explorable by workflow, stack, time-to-output, revision count, and review burden.
Interactive_Assets.SEO_or_discovery_benefit: Adds a discoverable data asset that can be cited.
Interactive_Assets.Conversion_or_retention_benefit: Creates a reason to return as new benchmark slices are added.
Interactive_Assets.Underlying_data_or_content_model: Benchmark rows, methodology metadata, update logs, downloadable CSVs, filter facets.
Interactive_Assets.Crawlable_fallback_requirement: Publish summary tables, methodology notes, and downloadable data links in crawlable HTML.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: People-first content, firsthand expertise, and originality principles used throughout the matrix.
Sources.Notes: Use as the governing filter for every scalable recommendation.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: Scaled-content abuse caution for programmatic SEO recommendations.
Sources.Notes: Used to justify selective indexing and anti-thin-content guidance.
Useful for the IA and technical hygiene layer.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: Why structured data helps Google understand pages and eligibility for rich results.
Article schema recommendation for essays, guides, and case studies.
Sources.Notes: Primary schema for most editorial pages.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: Breadcrumb markup guidance for site hierarchy and navigation.
Sources.Notes: Supports clean IA and rich-result eligibility.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: ProfilePage guidance for author/about pages that expose first-hand perspective.
Sources.Notes: Important for a personal authority site.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: Dataset markup guidance for downloadable resources, benchmarks, and structured assets.
Sources.Notes: Used for resource and benchmark page recommendations.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: VideoObject guidance for narrated walkthroughs or motion-led explainers.
Sources.Notes: Useful when interactive/animated assets also have watch pages.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: SoftwareApplication guidance for calculators/diagnostics where appropriate.
Sources.Notes: Used carefully for tool-like interactive assets.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: QAPage boundaries and when Q&A markup is appropriate.
Sources.Notes: Useful to avoid mis-marking editorial essays as Q&A pages.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: FAQ rich-result restrictions: only health/government authoritative sites.
Sources.Notes: Used to avoid over-recommending FAQPage markup on rajeevg.com.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: Canonicalization guidance for selective programmatic templates.
Current site structure, existing posts, and evidence of topical direction.
Sources.Notes: Used to anchor recommendations in current site reality.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: Existing proof asset for analytics-engineering authority.
Sources.Notes: Core trust-transfer page inside the matrix.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: Existing point-of-view page aligned with business-value messaging.
Sources.Notes: Useful flagship authority page to expand.
Supports builder credibility and case-study linking.

Sources.How_used_in_this_workbook: Existing project proof page tied to analytics implementation.
Sources.Notes: Useful as a concise proof surface linked from editorial content.
